summary: The number of people living in shelter as part of families with children more than doubled between FY22 and FY24, which DHS's own reporting attributes to the wave of asylum-seeking families that began arriving in the city in spring 2022. Growth essentially stopped in FY25 (61,103 to 60,805) as entries slowed and the agency accelerated exits to permanent housing.
oneLiner: increasing overall, but growth stalled in FY25 as entries slowed
